lessons.title: Ethical Issues in Biology and Biotechnology

content.body: BIOETHICS examines moral and ethical questions arising from biological research and its applications. As science advances, society must weigh benefits against risks and ethical concerns.

GENETIC ENGINEERING ETHICS:
• Benefits: cures for genetic diseases, more food, medicines
• Concerns: 'designer babies', genetic inequality, 'playing God', unpredictable consequences
• GM crops debate: higher yield vs biodiversity loss, corporate monopoly on seed patents

CLONING ETHICS:
• Therapeutic cloning: cloning embryos for stem cells to treat disease (accepted by many)
• Reproductive cloning: creating whole humans (banned in most countries)
• Animal cloning for agriculture vs animal welfare concerns

STEM CELL RESEARCH:
• Embryonic stem cells: high potential but involves destroying an embryo (controversial)
• Adult/iPSC stem cells: less controversial, limited potential
• Potential: treating Parkinson's, diabetes, spinal injuries

ANIMAL EXPERIMENTATION:
• The 3Rs: Replace (use alternatives), Reduce (use fewer animals), Refine (minimise suffering)
• Needed for drug safety testing vs animal rights arguments

PRIVACY AND GENETICS:
• Genetic screening — benefits (disease prevention) vs insurance/employment discrimination risks

questions.question: Bioethics is best defined as:
options: The study of all living organisms
Examination of moral/ethical issues arising from biological research
questions.answer: Examination of moral/ethical issues arising from biological research
questions.explanation: Bioethics bridges biology and ethics — asking 'just because we can, should we?' in scientific research.

questions.question: The '3Rs' in animal experimentation stand for:
questions.answer: Replace, Reduce, Refine
questions.explanation: 3Rs: Replace animals with alternatives where possible. Reduce numbers used. Refine methods to minimise suffering.

questions.question: Therapeutic cloning is controversial because:
options: It involves creating and destroying embryos for stem cells
questions.answer: It involves creating and destroying embryos for stem cells
questions.explanation: Therapeutic cloning creates embryos to harvest embryonic stem cells — many people believe embryos have moral status from conception.

questions.question: A main concern about GM crops is:
options: Possible unintended ecosystem effects and corporate control of food supply
questions.answer: Possible unintended ecosystem effects and corporate control of food supply
questions.explanation: GM genes could spread to wild plants; dominant companies owning seed patents could create dependency in poor farmers.

questions.question: Germline gene editing (changing genes in embryos) is controversial because:
options: Changes are heritable — affecting all future generations without their consent
questions.answer: Changes are heritable — affecting all future generations without their consent
questions.explanation: Editing embryo genes changes the human germline permanently. Future children and descendants cannot consent.

questions.question: Embryonic stem cells are ethically controversial because:
options: Obtaining them requires destroying a human embryo
questions.answer: Obtaining them requires destroying a human embryo
questions.explanation: Embryonic stem cells are harvested from 5-day-old blastocysts — this destroys the embryo. Many consider this morally wrong.

questions.question: Induced Pluripotent Stem Cells (iPSCs) are less controversial than embryonic stem cells because:
options: They are derived from adult cells, not embryos
questions.answer: They are derived from adult cells, not embryos
questions.explanation: iPSCs are made by reprogramming adult (e.g. skin) cells back to a stem cell state — no embryo is destroyed.

questions.question: The main argument FOR animal testing in medicine is:
options: It is necessary to ensure drug safety before human trials
questions.answer: It is necessary to ensure drug safety before human trials
questions.explanation: Animal testing identifies toxic compounds before human exposure — has prevented many harmful drugs reaching humans.

questions.question: Genetic screening of a foetus raises ethical concerns about:
options: Possible termination of pregnancy based on genetic information
questions.answer: Possible termination of pregnancy based on genetic information
questions.explanation: Prenatal genetic testing may lead to abortion of foetuses with genetic conditions — raises serious ethical debates.

questions.question: 'Designer babies' refers to:
options: Selecting or editing embryo genes for desired traits
questions.answer: Selecting or editing embryo genes for desired traits
questions.explanation: Designer babies: using PGD or gene editing to select/alter traits (disease resistance, intelligence, appearance) — highly controversial.

questions.question: Biodiversity concerns about GM monocultures relate to:
options: Reduction in genetic diversity and wild plant relatives due to single crop varieties
GM crops being toxic to humans
questions.answer: Reduction in genetic diversity and wild plant relatives due to single crop varieties
questions.explanation: Mass planting of identical GM varieties reduces genetic diversity, making food supply vulnerable to a single disease.

questions.question: The precautionary principle in bioethics says:
options: If potential harm is severe and uncertain, action to prevent harm should not wait for full proof
questions.answer: If potential harm is severe and uncertain, action to prevent harm should not wait for full proof
questions.explanation: Precautionary principle: when risks are uncertain but potentially catastrophic, take preventive measures first.

questions.question: Human reproductive cloning is:
options: Banned in most countries due to ethical and safety concerns
questions.answer: Banned in most countries due to ethical and safety concerns
questions.explanation: No country currently permits reproductive human cloning due to both ethical objections and severe technical safety risks.

questions.question: Insurance companies accessing genetic test results could cause:
options: Genetic discrimination — higher premiums or denial of cover for those with risk genes
questions.answer: Genetic discrimination — higher premiums or denial of cover for those with risk genes
questions.explanation: If insurers know your genetic risks, they may charge more or refuse coverage — creating a genetically disadvantaged class.

questions.question: The benefit of genetic screening for diseases like sickle cell anaemia is:
options: Eliminates the disease from the population
Allows informed reproductive decisions and early treatment
questions.answer: Allows informed reproductive decisions and early treatment
questions.explanation: Genetic counselling using screening gives carriers information to make reproductive choices and prepare for potential illness.

questions.question: Bioprospecting (finding new drugs in biodiversity) raises ethical issues because:
options: Indigenous knowledge is sometimes used without compensation or consent
questions.answer: Indigenous knowledge is sometimes used without compensation or consent
questions.explanation: Companies sometimes patent traditional remedies from indigenous communities without sharing benefits — biopiracy.

questions.question: The main argument AGAINST reproductive human cloning is:
options: Creates individuals with same genome as another person, undermining individuality; severe health risks
questions.answer: Creates individuals with same genome as another person, undermining individuality; severe health risks
questions.explanation: Dolly the sheep showed cloned animals age prematurely. Human cloning would likely have severe defects; ethical problems with 'copying' a person.

questions.question: Stem cells are valuable in medicine because they:
options: Can differentiate into many cell types and potentially replace damaged tissues
questions.answer: Can differentiate into many cell types and potentially replace damaged tissues
questions.explanation: Pluripotent stem cells can become any cell type — potential to regenerate damaged heart, nerve, or pancreatic cells.

questions.question: Informed consent in biological research means:
options: Participants are fully informed of risks and benefits and freely agree to participate
questions.answer: Participants are fully informed of risks and benefits and freely agree to participate
questions.explanation: Informed consent is a fundamental bioethical principle — people must understand and voluntarily agree to research participation.

questions.question: The CRISPR-Cas9 gene editing tool raised global alarm in 2018 when it was used to:
options: Edit the genes of human embryos that were then implanted and born as babies
questions.answer: Edit the genes of human embryos that were then implanted and born as babies
questions.explanation: Chinese scientist He Jiankui created gene-edited babies (CCR5 deletion) in 2018 — condemned worldwide as premature and unethical.
